English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Robertson family papers
Robertson family.
852
.5 cubic feet.
1820-1907. Letters, school records, painting. Correspondence and other papers of the Robertson family of Washington County, New York, and several other related families. The papers of Smith Robertson consist of his letters written while a student at Union College (Schenectady, New York) and as superintendent of Common Schools in Tompkins County; also as a medical student at Geneva Medical College, at Yale University, and while traveling to Washington, D.C.; a painting by F. E. Robertson (brother of Mary "Grandma" Moses). Robertson family genealogy; also material on related families, including Bishop, Brown, Houtz, King, Snyder, Teeter, and Wood. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 1 28 - folder 1 28 - folder 1 1 (positive) ======= 1 28 - folder 1 28 - folder 1 1 (positive) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2098582
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
K.J. Ginage
Date completed:
December, 1970
EAD encoding:
Peter Martinez, January 2003
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, November 2015
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Correspondence and other papers of the Robertson family of Washington County, New York, and several other related families, dealing mostly with family matters and the migration westward of family members to Ohio, Missouri, Minnesota, and California. The papers of Smith Robertson consist of his letters written while a student at Union College (Schenectady, New York) and as superintendent of Common Schools in Tompkins County; also as a medical student at Geneva Medical College, at Yale University, and while traveling to Washington, D.C. Chief subjects include education, medicine, travel, and personal affairs. Correspondents include Horace Mann, Henry Barnard, and Douglass Boardman; he also describes meeting John Quincy Adams. Also, a painting by F. E. Robertson (brother of Mary "Grandma" Moses). Robertson family genealogy, including data on George Robertson (born in Scotland and lived in Dryden, New York); Smith Robertson, Hiram Robertson (husband of Mary Cornell); also material on related families, including Bishop, Brown, Houtz, King, Snyder, Teeter, and Wood.
RELATED MATERIALS
Includes collections #184, #243, #908, and #852. Guide was originally created for collection #243
SERIES LIST
Series I. Correspondence Collection #243, Box 1: Folders 1-12
September 1840 - December 1841 January- October 1842 January 29 - October 7, 1843 January 9 - April 16, 1844 May 27 - December 31, 1844 January 10 - July 18, 1845 August 11 - November 13, 1845 January 27 - July 28, 1846 August 12 - October 26, 1846 February 19 - October 14, 1847 January 16, 1848 - June 30, 1852 Undated
Series II. Miscellaneous Letters and Receipts (not involving SR) 1821 - 1850 Collection #243, Box 1: Folder 13
Series III. Miscellaneous Madison and Chenango County School District Records Collection #243, Box 1: Folder 14
Cite As:
Robertson family papers, #852. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Washington (D.C.) -- Description and travel. Washington County (N.Y.) -- Genealogy. Families -- New York (State) -- Washington County. Migration, Internal -- California. Migration, Internal -- Minnesota. Migration, Internal -- Missouri. Migration, Internal -- Ohio. Medicine -- Study and teaching -- New York (State) -- Geneva. Schools -- Administration -- New York (State) -- Tompkins County. Education -- New York (State) -- Ithaca. Teachers colleges -- New York (State) Medical education -- New York (State) -- Geneva. Robertson, Smith. Robertson, Hiram. Robertson, F. E. Moses, Grandma, 1860-1961. Adams, John Quincy, 1767-1848. Mann, Horace, 1796-1859. Barnard, Henry. Boardman, Douglass, 1822-1891. Bishop family. Brown family. Houtz family. King family. Snyder family. Cornell, Mary. Teeters family. Wood family. Union College (Schenectady, N.Y.) -- : Students. Geneva Medical College (Geneva, N.Y.) -- : Students.
2098582 <<<<<<< HEAD This Guide is Limited to the Contents of Former Collection #243Series I. CorrespondenceSeptember 1840 - December 1841 (10 items)From Smith Robertson (SR) at Union College, Schenectady, to his parents, Mr. and Mrs. George Robertson, at VarnaSeptember 5, 1840243 11
Scope and Contents
Concerned with the College. (SR member of July 1843 graduating class.)SR, Union, from E. Canfield, SandisfieldNovember 15, 1840243 11From W. Smith at Yale to SR at Union; is concerned with life at Yale; courses; death of a friendNovember 29, 1840243 11From SR at Union to William H. Lacy at Cortland Academy, HomerNovember243 11
Scope and Contents
"Seward runs behind while Bouck runs far ahead of his ticket"; other political comments. Also, references to college life, studies, professors.From B. C. Whiting, Binghamton, to SRMarch 15, 1841243 11From O. S. Wood, Dryden, to SR at UnionMarch 25, 1841243 11Printed report form from Union; addressed to George RobertsonApril [6] 1841 (postmark)243 11
Scope and Contents
Includes tuition bill and marks for term January 8-April 1, 1841; also general information on courses, admission requirements, etc.From Ruel Smith, RochesterMay 21, 1841243 11
Scope and Contents
Who is studying medicine in the office of a professor of surgery at Geneva Medical College; mentions practices of medical students who procured corpses already buried for their dissections. To SR at Union.Term report from Union as in April aboveAugust 11, 1841243 11
Scope and Contents
For period April 30-July 28, 1841.From Cousin Molly, Albany, to SR at UnionDecember 9, 1841243 11January- October 1842 (11 items)From SR at Union College to William H. Lacy, CortlandvilleJanuary 8243 12
Scope and Contents
Remarks about newly elected state legislators; plans for present term at College and future entering of Law School.To SR at Union from N. B. Smith, IthacaFebruary 21, 1842243 12Term report from Union for period January 7-April 1, 1842April 9 (postmark)243 12From William Smith at Yale to SR at UnionMay 12 or 13 (sic)1842243 12From Howard Townsend, Albany, to SR at UnionMay 29, 1842243 12
Scope and Contents
Tells of H's journey to Washington, D.C.; his meeting John Quincy Adams, Daniel Webster, hearing Henry Clay speak, dining at the White House with President Tyler, and seeing the sights of the Capital.From James Wood, Jr., Union College, to SR in DryclenJuly 8, 1842243 12
Scope and Contents
About an overpayment of a debt; SR now in a lawyer's office.From Isaac Mott to SR at UnionJuly 12, 1842(postmark)243 12To SR from a cousin. Tells of the illness of his Aunt SallyJuly 25, 1842243 12Term report from Union for period April 29th-July 27, 1842August 10, 1842 (postmark)243 12From Cyrus Robertson in Albion, Michigan, to his brother, SRSeptember 5, 1842243 12
Scope and Contents
Concern about worth of Michigan land SR wishers to sellFrom Walter Mott, cousin of SROctober 12, 1842243 12
Scope and Contents
Invitation to SR to visit Mott in Albany at; the Medical College and perhaps see an operation performed.January 29 - October 7, 1843 (9 items)From Howard Townsend at Union to SR in EtnaJanuary 29, 1843243 13From Mitchell (fraternity brother of SR?), "Ike" and others to SRFebruary 1843243 13From N. R. Biddlecomb, and others to SRMarch 31, 1843243 13
Scope and Contents
Urging his return to Union College and reporting events since his departure.From State of New York, Secretary's Office, Department of Common Schools Albany to SR- -Superintendent of Common Schools, Tompkins Co., IthacaApril 10, 1843243 13
Scope and Contents
Regarding a law of 1843 stating that all appeals presented to the State Secretary's Office must have first been submitted to and had a decision rendered by the superintendent of schools in the particular county.From M. Scofield, Whitesboro, to SRApril 16, 1843243 13From SR at Union College to parentsMay 1, 1843243 13
Scope and Contents
Regarding his return to CollegeFrom D. R. Houts, Ithaca, to SR at Union CollegeMay 23, 1843243 13From Hiram D. Robertson at Albion, Michigan, to Parents (George Robertson) about trip to DetroitMay 23, 1843243 13From Asa Barton, Union College to SR at Varna, Tompkins CoOctober 7, 1843243 13
Scope and Contents
About news of SR's fraternity at Union, Sigma Phi.January 9 - April 16, 1844 (11 items)From W. Smith at Yale University to SR, Varna, Tompkins Co., N.YJanuary 9, 1844243 14
Scope and Contents
Some facts about life at Yale and news of friendsFrom S. Town of Aurora to SRJanuary 18, 1844243 14
Scope and Contents
About Town help including Robertson with a "pioneer enterprise thro' which the cause of primary education will receive so much aid."From H. Gillam of Aurora to SR about the possible visit of a Mr. TownFebruary 15, 1844243 14From S. Town of Aurora to SRFebruary 27, 1844243 14
Scope and Contents
Says that a visit to Tompkins Co. would be impossible. Town speaks of a project undertaken by Tompkins Co. teachers that has spread to other parts of the State; he commends the venture and wishes them success in the future.From A. Potter, Union College, to SR, IthacaMarch 6, 1844243 14
Scope and Contents
Potter isn't sure whether or not he will be able to attend an Institute.From S. Young, Secretary's Office, Department of Common Schools, Albany to SR, Superintendent of Tompkins Co. Common SchoolsMarch 11, 1844243 14
Scope and Contents
Regarding teacher certification for a Mr. Curtis and commending SR on the reputation of the Teachers' InstituteFrom D. A. Morton, town clerk, Groton, to SR, Superintendent Common Schools of Tompkins CountyMarch 11, 1844243 14
Scope and Contents
Listing names of district clerks for the Groton Common Schools.From Eleaser Carter of Caroline, to SRMarch 20, 1844243 14
Scope and Contents
Informing SR that the trustees have refused his (SR's) request for a meeting. Carter says that he will arrange for SR to review the clerk's books and will give SR all the help that he can.From Cyrus Clark and John S. Holden to SR as Superintendent of Tompkins Co. Common SchoolsMarch 21, 1844243 14
Scope and Contents
A notice of an appeal being sent to the N.Y.S. Superintendent of Schools concerning the Board of Trustees' refusal to comply with an existing statute. SR. had ruled in favor of the Trustees.George B. Glendening, Cayuga Academy, Aurora to SRMarch 27, 1844243 14
Scope and Contents
Acceptance of invitation to visit SR.From S. Young, State of New York Secretary's Office, Dept of Common. Schools, to SRMarch 30, 1844243 14
Scope and Contents
State Superintendent upholds decision of SR in the case of Holden and Clark vs. the Trustees of the town, of Lansing District #7, saying that trustees have right to levy tax.From Douglass Boardman, Trumansburg, to SRApril 3, 1844243 14
Scope and Contents
Letter tells of the electioneering done by Boardman's opponents in an election in the attempts to get another man elected.From S. Town, Angelica, to SRApril 16, 1844243 14
Scope and Contents
Reports on the Teacher's Institute in Angelica.May 27 - December 31, 1844 (14 items)To SR from. S.D. Carr of GrotonMay 27, 1844243 15
Scope and Contents
A man in the school district refuses to pay taxes on land which is not in said district.From Douglass Boardman, Trumansburg, to SRJune 4, 1844243 15
Scope and Contents
Requesting that SR send the dates he will be able to visit Trumansburg.Printed invitation from Alpha of the Sigma Phi with note from How(ard?) PorterJune 29, 1844243 15To SR from S. D. CarrJuly 29, 1844243 15
Scope and Contents
Asking that SR visit the Groton district schools and commenting on the next school term.From Lyman Cobb, N.Y.C. to SRAugust 12, 1844243 15
Scope and Contents
About text books being considered by the Books Committee of the County School Association.From S. Young, Secretary's Office, Dept. of Common Schools, Albany, to SRAugust 13, 1844243 15
Scope and Contents
Granting the request for an extension in handing in the district's annual report and commending the evaluation SR is doing of the Common Schools in Tompkins Co.From Douglass Boardman, Trumansburg, to SRAugust 13, 1844243 15
Scope and Contents
The State Superintendent has asked that each district send in the number and description of the books in the school libraries. Boardman also reports that a district meeting will soon take place to discuss new school sites and taxes.From S. Young, Secretary's Office, Department of Common Schools, Albany to SRSeptember 4, 1844243 15
Scope and Contents
The state regulation calling for a catalogue of books held by each district must be followed.To SR from D. W. Carley, NewfieldSeptember 21, 1844243 15
Scope and Contents
Carley tells of his plans for the ending of the school term and the examination of his pupils.From S. Town to SR. Town "will come to Ithaca."October 3, 1844243 15From E. Powell, Owego, to SROctober 17, 1844243 15
Scope and Contents
Letter sends news of Teacher Institute.From S. D. Carr, Groton, to SROctober 21, 1844243 15
Scope and Contents
Letter about the possible hiring of a Mr. Everson in SR's district.From Alex Holland, Union Coll., to SRDecember 3, 1844243 15
Scope and Contents
Acknowledgment payment of money to Union College.From Horace Mann, Boston, to SRDecember 31, 1844243 15
Scope and Contents
Can not meet with SR until following April. Commends SR for his (SR's) new work in New York State common schools.January 10 - July 18, 1845 (15 items)From P. A. Ely, Hector, to SR, Superintendent Tompkins Co. Common SchoolsJanuary 10, 1845243 16
Scope and Contents
A request of SR can not be fulfilled and Ely does not, have a candidate for the Normal School.From Robert Alien, Joseph Ammack, Samuel Alien, Jonathan Owen, Woodard White, and John Fish, all of Enfield, to SR, Superintendent of Tompkins Co. Common SchoolsJanuary 15, 1845243 16
Scope and Contents
Letter is an appeal to SR in behalf of a poor family in the school district. The District Board of Trustees has demanded the payment of the rate bill by this man who does not have the money. The petitioners are asking SR to exempt this man from the rate bill.From S. Town of Aurora to SR, Supt. Tompkins Co. Common SchoolMarch 8, 1845243 16
Scope and Contents
S. Town can not accept invitation of SR.From E. G. Starke, Auburn, to SRMarch 10, 1845243 16
Scope and Contents
A letter which SR mailed to Starke had been sent to the wrong address and the event which Starke had been invited to, had already taken place. Starke is angered by a movement in Albany seeking to abolish County Superintendents. He attacks Silas Wright, U.S. Senator 1833; reelected 1837 and 1843; governor 1845-47, as being the American Cato and states that Wright has not the information needed to make the statements he has been making. Starke comes out against "bigotry and ignorance coupled with rank injustice in high stations."From Edwin Cooper, Waterloo Academy, to SRMarch 15, 1845243 16
Scope and Contents
Cooper can not "during his vacation" visit SR but will be in Ithaca for two days during the next institute. He praises SR's work and comments on the effect it has upon other counties.From S. Young, Ballston, to SR, IthacaMarch 25, 1845243 16
Scope and Contents
The letter is an answer to an invitation of SR to Young. Young talks about the prejudices of certain segments of the community against the school system. Young says that education is not for the rich alone, as the mass of people think, but for the poor as well. No one should be denied the opportunity of receiving an education. '"No philanthropist should remit his exertions until our Schools are raised to such a grade of utility as to embrace all the children of the State, the sons and daughters of the rich as well as of the poor. All should here mingle on one common level; all should be blended into one mass during the susceptible period of youth. This is the only means of promoting the harmony of society, of suppressing jealousies and perpetuating in letter and in spirit our republican institutions."From Edwin Cooper, Waterloo to SR, IthacaApril 7, 1845243 16
Scope and Contents
Cooper will not be able to attend the Teacher Institute in Ithaca because of the illness of his wife.From Cyrus Robertson, Albion, Michigan, to his brother Smith Robertson, IthacaMay 4, 1845243 16>Lovel (?) Preston, Caroline, to SRMay 30, 1845243 16
Scope and Contents
Letter is praising SR for his system of school instruction.Printed folder - written by Franklin KnightJuly 1, 1845243 16
Scope and Contents
The folder is a sort of advertisement for a book of facsimiles of letter from George Washington on agriculture Knight has published that will "have a most salutary influence in correcting, in the youth of our country, many false notions regarding the character of agricultural pursuits."Letter from the publisher, Alexander V. Blake, N.Y.C. to SRJuly 1, 1845243 16
Scope and Contents
Printed circular for Wickham's "Educational Incentives," particularly Teacher's Tokens with facsimile. Letter tells SR about the invention of a School Ledger which would enable a teacher to keep permanent records of students.From I (?) Cooper McLean, N.Y. , to SRJuly 2, 1845243 16
Scope and Contents
A new certificate is to be made out for Miss Samantha Burton enabling her to teach in any school district in Tompkins' County.R. Hyde Walworth, President, New York Juvenile Temperance Association to SR and all County and town superintendentsJuly 4, 1845243 16
Scope and Contents
Printed circular urging them to promote said association and its goals in their district or county.From Francis Dwight, Albany, to SRJuly 7, 1845243 16
Scope and Contents
A Mr. D. P. Page will be in the area in the middle of September and will be willing to address a Teacher Institute if there is one at that time.From Henry McGonegal of AlbanyJuly 18, 1845243 16
Scope and Contents
Asking SR for a recommendation. McGonegal is seeking a position in Syracuse as a teacher.August 11 - November 13, 1845 (10 items)From Albert D. Wright, Verona to SR, IthacaAugust 11, 1845243 17
Scope and Contents
Dwight had promised SR that he would speak at the Tompkins County Teachers' Institute. He is asking to be released from this engagement since he is being asked by his own district (county) to be in charge of an Institute there.From E. C. Seymour, Albany, to SR, IthacaAugust 11, 1845243 17
Scope and Contents
Letter informs that Seymour will be leaving the school at the end of the term. Professors Perkins and Page will not be able to attend the Tompkins County Institute. Seymour asks the help of SR in finding a job for the next term.Circular giving details of the coming Cayuga County Teachers' InstituteAugust 13, 1845243 17From Henry Barnard of Providence to SR, IthacaSeptember 14, 1845243 17
Scope and Contents
Barnard will be able to attend the Institute and will deliver an address on "What can be done to improve the schools this winter?"From Henry Barnard,Providence, to SR, IthacaOctober 5, 1845243 17
Scope and Contents
The letter confirms Barnard's appearance at the Teachers' Institute, October 14 and announces his plans for an earlier arrival.From Edwin Cooper, Syracuse, to SR, IthacaOctober 22, 1845243 17
Scope and Contents
Cooper will not be able to attend the Teachers' Institute. He also encourages SR to accept a copy of a "current volume of the Advocates'" and to encourage the teachers in his district to become subscribers.From O. O. Wickham, N.Y.C. to SROctober 27, 1845243 17
Scope and Contents
Wickham will not be able to attend the Institute and present his "Educational Incentives" to the teachers present. However, he is sending samples and asks that a wall chart called School Chart of Characteristics be placed on the wall of some room at the Institute.From Charles R. Coburn, Owego, to SROctober 30, 1845243 17
Scope and Contents
Inviting SR and Mr. Barnard to the Owego Institute and Esq. Gillet, if he can be spared. He invites SR to speak to them about education.From Daniel Jackson, Burdett, to SRNovember 8, 1845243 17
Scope and Contents
About a teacher, Mr. J. W. Carpenter, then teaching at his school, who had the previous year been in trouble over a young woman of the town whom he had since married. Jackson says that except for that one incident, Carpenter's reputation and behavior had been very fine.From O. O. Wickham, N.Y.C. to SRNovember 13, 1845243 17
Scope and Contents
Asking for the names of all of those present at the last Teachers' Institute so that he may advertise his Teacher's Book of Instrumentalities and School Requisites.January 27 - July 28, 1846 (12 items)From Thomas Slater, Albany, to SRJanuary 27, 1846243 18
Scope and Contents
Professor Perkins will be able to attend the Institute planned for the spring.From H. Camp, school trustee in Trumansburg, to SR, Supt. of Common Schools, Tompkins CountyFebruary 17, 1846243 18
Scope and Contents
In regard to a notice that there will be a public examination of students in the near future. Camp says, on behalf of the other trustees, that their school will not be taking part in the examination.From C. S. Ford, Geneva to SRMarch 19, 1846243 18
Scope and Contents
About Ford's appearance as a lecturer at a future Teachers' Institute.From S. Town, Aurora to SRMarch 26, 1846243 18
Scope and Contents
Town cannot, at the time, indicate whether or not he will be able to visit Ithaca.From G. H. Stevens, Danby, to SRApril 8, 1846243 18
Scope and Contents
Sherman Miller has been newly elected Town Superintendent of Schools. Canfield and Stevens are going to School [to keep school?]From Doug(lass) (Boardman) Trumansburg, to SR as Superintendent of Common Schools, Tompkins CoMay 20, 1846243 18
Scope and Contents
Giving exact time of his arrival in Ithaca.SR from T. Russell Jencks of BostonMay 26, 1846243 18From Palmer and Jencks, Boston, to SR, Supt. of Common SchoolsJune 15, 1846243 18
Scope and Contents
They have found the journals he enquired about and wish to know if he wants them to be sent to him.Printed circular from N. S. Benton, Supt., N.Y.S., Secretary's Office, Dept. of Common SchoolsJuly 1, 1846243 18
Scope and Contents
A previous circular should be disregarded if it does not apply to a particular Superintendent,.From J. S. (or I. S.) Mitchell, Canajoharie, to SRJuly 10, 1846243 18From Superintendent of Caroline to SRJuly 28, 1846243 18
Scope and Contents
Reporting on the salary of the County and town superintendent of schools note attached re: amount of compensation for Dr. Farlin [See Ace. #2241]. Town Superintendent.August 12 - October 26, 1846 (11 items)From Jacob Abbott of New York to SRAugust 12, 1846243 19
Scope and Contents
Abbott is not certain whether or not he will be able to attend an Institute scheduled for early October. He thanks SR for the positive information and says that he will send a definite answer when he is able to.From C. La. (?) Ford, Medina to SR of IthacaAugust 15, 1846243 19
Scope and Contents
Ford is not certain whether or not he will be able to speak on Anatomy and Physiology at an Institute in the early part of OctoberFrom A.J. Tompkins of Lansing inviting SR to their school celebrationSeptember 11, 1846243 19From Alonzo Wynkoop, Chemung, to SR of IthacaSeptember 21, 1846243 19
Scope and Contents
Wynkoop asks for SR's assistance in finding qualified teachers to lead an Institute.From Horace Mann, Boston to SRSeptember 26, 1846243 19
Scope and Contents
Mann sends his regrets and says he will not be able to attend their Institute. He commends SR for the work he is doing.Printed circular from Andrews and Boyle, Publishers, N.Y.C. to SRSeptember 26, 1846243 19
Scope and Contents
Requesting the names and mailing addresses of all the town superintendents in the county and all of the teachers in the county. Andrews and Boyle are publishing a weekly newspaper, the Anglo Saxon, and hope to interest the superintendents and teachers in becoming subscribers.From Jacob Abbott, N.Y., to SROctober 5, 1846243 19
Scope and Contents
Abbott will be able to visit the Institute.Printed circular from 0. C. Gardiner of N.Y.C. to SR, Supt. of Common Schools, Tompkins CountyOctober 8, 1846243 19
Scope and Contents
Circular is a testimonial for the magazine American Quarterly Journal of Agriculture and Science; hand written letter {pages 2-3) is sent to encourage SR to subscribe to magazine.From Oliver Dyer of New York to SROctober 8, 1846243 19
Scope and Contents
Mr. Augustus Boyle will not be able to address 'the Teachers' Institute on Phonographics and sends his regrets.From P. S. Ely, Hector, to SR of IthacaOctober 16, 1846243 19
Scope and Contents
Ely's visit to Ithaca has been postponed a week. The Burdett School is in need of a teacher and Ely suggests that SR send them one.Form letter from N. S. Benton, Supt. Common Schools, Secretary's Office, Dept. of Common Schools, Albany to County Superintendent SROctober 26, 1846243 19
Scope and Contents
Blank reports for district trustees are available.February 19 - October 14, 1847 (11 items)Form letter from Delphian Institute of Union College, Schenectady to SR as a former memberFebruary 19, 1847243 110
Scope and Contents
Asking for a donation of either money or books to keep, in the Institute's attempt to fit up a reading room.From Alonzo Wynkoop, County Supt. of Chemung, to SR, IthacaMarch 15, 1847243 110
Scope and Contents
Three counties, Tompkins, Steuben and Chemung are planning a union Teachers' Institute to which all the neighboring counties are being invited. The letter from Wynkoop is to inform SR of what has been planned so far; who the principal speakers will be, a sample of the notice to appear in the newspapers (handwritten).From Alonzo Wynkoop, Chemung, to SR, IthacaApril 10, 1847243 110
Scope and Contents
Letter gives SR more news about the union Institute informing him that notices have been sent to newspapers. Wynkoop has had no definite answer from those whom he has asked to address the Institute, If all else fails, he will call upon SR to deliver an address. (first mention is made of Robertson being married as Wynkoop says " . . .and shall hope to meet your bride,. . . .")From N. S. Benton, State of New York Secretary's Office, Dept. of Common Schools, Albany to SRMay 6, 1847243 110
Scope and Contents
Letter says that land is liable to assessment by a district if it lies partially within that district and if the owner/occupant resides on the land in that particular district.From B. F. Winegar, a member of SR's fraternity, Sigma Phi, at Union College, to SRJune 23, 1847243 110
Scope and Contents
The fraternity needs donations from members to build a membership hall. This letter contains second mention of SR having recently married.From Cyrus Robertson, Albion, Michigan, to his brother, SRJuly 24, 1847243 110Printed circular from N. S. Benton, Secretary's Office, Dept. of Common Schools, Albany, to County Supt. SRSeptember 1, 1847243 110
Scope and Contents
The circular contains information pertaining to the reduction of the amount of gratuity allowed to pupils.From Benj[amin] Cook, Penn Yan to SROctober 4, 1847243 110
Scope and Contents
Cook will not be able to attend the whole of an Institute to lecture and teach Phonography but will be present for the second week.From J. S. (?) Holmes, Deputy Supt. of Common Schools, State of New York, Secretary's Office, Dept. of Common Schools, to SROctober 7, 1847243 110
Scope and Contents
Letter concerns a discrepancy concerning sums of money received during the current year, which appear in SR's abstract of Town Superintendent Reports.From James Redfield of Lyons, County Supt. for Wayne County to SROctober 14, 1847243 110
Scope and Contents
Introducing Dr. Calvin Cutler, author of a book on physiology; on same sheet.From Calvin Cutler, Ithaca to SROctober 19, 1847243 110
Scope and Contents
Sending SR a copy of the above mentioned book.January 16, 1848 - June 30, 1852From Philip and Sarah Robertson, Conneaut, Ohio, to SRJanuary 16, 1848243 111From B. R. King & P. R. King, Middleport, N.Y. to their brother and sister, SR and ? (his wife?) sleighing; RR correspondenceMarch 26, 1848243 111From W. Van Wyck, Union College to SRMarch 27, 1848243 111
Scope and Contents
The letter asks for funds for the finishing of the Conclave hall for the Sigma Phi fraternity at Union College.From H. (?) McGonegal, Baldwinsville, to SRJuly 11, 1848243 111
Scope and Contents
Asking SR for a recommendation for a teaching position.From O. O. Wickham, Clinton Hall (pm Williamsburg) , N.Y. to SRNovember 16, 1849243 111
Scope and Contents
Wickham requests a list of the names and addresses of the teachers in the area so that he may send them ". . .some valuable information which will be to their advantage and advance the cause of education."Printed circular from Association of Former Students of Cortland Academy to SRJune 30, 1852243 111
Scope and Contents
Asks for donations for a fund to be given to Mr. Wollworth, by his former pupils upon his resignation.Undated (5 items)From Thomas (?) Dwight, Albany, to SRJuly 17243 112
Scope and Contents
Concerns an Institute not being held. There is also mention made of an attack upon the present school system being made at a teachers' convention.From Thomas (?) Dwight, Albany, to SRAugust 2243 112
Scope and Contents
Dwight is asking SR for information of the upcoming Tompkins County Institute.From Peter Selover, Homer, to SRNovember 4243 112
Scope and Contents
The author of the letter had been promised a letter he did not receive. He is now appealing to SR for a certificate and/or a letter of recommendation.Printed circular advertising an arithmetic seriesUndated243 112Printed circular advertising Mitchell's Map EstablishmentUndated243 112Miscellaneous Letters and Receipts (not involving Smith Robertson) 1821 - 1850 (20 items)1821 - 1850From Adjt. General W. L. Marcy, Troy, to General Samuel Hatheway, HomerOctober 29, 1821243 113
Scope and Contents
General Marcy has been asked to give an opinion about whether or not officers who did not parade their companies on a certain required date should be punished. If they did not know that they were required to perform a duty, then they can not be punished for failing to fulfill the obligation. Note: Xerox copy placed in Hatheway Family Papers, Ace. #2281.Receipt for pay as teacher in District #4, town and county unnamed; signed by Israel RobertsApril 28, 1828243 113From Ebby Hyde, Farmer [Interlaken], to Doctor Frederick Hyde, Cortland VillageMarch 4, 1836243 113
Scope and Contents
Augustus (?) has just left Ebby Hyde's after a visit. Ebby says that he regrets that Frederick could not have been there at the same time as Augustus but says it was not possible. Ebby talks of a trip he is soon to start upon and says he is looking forward to Frederick's visit.From G. Woodruff, Buffalo, to Dr. Frederick Hyde, CortlandvilleJuly 14, 1837243 113
Scope and Contents
Personal letter containing family news and other items of gossip. Woodruff teases Hyde about his (Hyde's) "unpatriotic bachelorhood."From C. F. Woodruff, Ithaca, to father (step-father?, father-in-law?) P. Pelton of Monticello, N.YJuly 20, 1842243 113
Scope and Contents
Regarding trip by stagecoach from Monticello to Ithaca.From J. W. French of Albion, New York, to Mr. E. B. Walsworth, of Walworth, Wayne CoDecember 18, 1843243 113
Scope and Contents
Concerning a report to be made out re the Walworth Academy of which Walsworth is principal.From James S. McRie, Union College, to E. B.. WalsworththJanuary 22, 1844243 113
Scope and Contents
Asking for a teaching position at the Walworth Academy where Walsworth is principal.From George Loomis of Genesee Wesleyan Seminary, Lima, Livingston Co. to E. B. WalsworthFebruary 21, 1844243 113
Scope and Contents
Saying that visit to "Lyceum" at Walworth, Wayne Co. at present would not be possible.From Joseph Rosenkrans of Fort Plain to Mr. Walsworth of Union CollegeMay 30, 1844243 113
Scope and Contents
Request that Walsworth deliver sermon in place of Rosenkrans while he (Rosenkrans) must be away.From Caleb Clark, Truxton, to Dr. Frederick Hyde, Cortland (ville)September 30, 1845243 113
Scope and Contents
Clark is ill and wishes Dr. Hyde to prescribe some medicine for him.From John Foster, Union College, to E. B. Walsworth at Auburn Theological SeminaryMay 26, 1846243 113
Scope and Contents
In doubt as to date of Auburn Commencement and "propriety" of his [Foster's] addressing the body.From Silas C. Brown, W. Bloomfield, to his nephew E. B. Walsworth, Auburn, N.Y. Family mattersJune 12, 1846243 113From Rev. E. G. Townsend, Sackets Harbor, to E. Walsworth, Auburn. Personal news and commentDecember 17, 1846243 113From H. W. Parker, Mexico, N.Y., to E. B. Walsworth, Auburn Theological SeminaryMarch 25, 1847243 113
Scope and Contents
(Rev.) Parker wishes someone to substitute for him for four Sabbaths and asks Walsworth's assistance in securing someone.From Albert West, Troy to Ira G. FellowsNovember 7, 1847243 113
Scope and Contents
Will be 21 11/8 (b. 1826). Visit to N.Y.C. - theatre-going, attending Trinity Church.From Albert West, Troy, to Ira G. Fellows, Pulaski. Mutual friends and daily activitiesMay 7, 1848243 113From Henry L. Young of Avon, to Rev. E. B. Walsworth, Union Theological Seminary, N.YMay 8, 1848243 113
Scope and Contents
Is invited to assume Presbyterian pastorate at Avon. "This is missionary ground. . . ."From Albert West, Troy, to Ira G. Fellows. Personal newsNovember 5, 1848243 113From Albert West, Troy, to Ira G. FellowsFebruary 4, 1850243 113
Scope and Contents
Social news -- sleigh rides.From Albert West, Troy, to Ira G. Fellows, PulaskiApril 12, 1850243 113
Scope and Contents
Maple sugar making news from California; "'tell the boys to let well enough alone and stay at home."Miscellaneous Madison and Chenango County School District Records (3 items)Warrant from Almon Baker, trustee of School District #8, Cazenovia, Madison County ordering him to receive school taxesJuly 13, 1884243 114
Scope and Contents
Includes a list of taxable inhabitants and amounts they owe. 1 item (2 pcs.)Order to Tax Collector to pay wages earned by Addie Warner as teacher in School District #9, Town of Lincklaen, Chenango CountyNovember 18, 1895243 114A handwritten catalogue of books (15 vols.) belonging to School District #2, Town of LincklaenUndated243 114 ======= This Guide is Limited to the Contents of Former Collection #243Series I. CorrespondenceSeptember 1840 - December 1841 (10 items)From Smith Robertson (SR) at Union College, Schenectady, to his parents, Mr. and Mrs. George Robertson, at VarnaSeptember 5, 1840243 11
Scope and Contents
Concerned with the College. (SR member of July 1843 graduating class.)SR, Union, from E. Canfield, SandisfieldNovember 15, 1840243 11From W. Smith at Yale to SR at Union; is concerned with life at Yale; courses; death of a friendNovember 29, 1840243 11From SR at Union to William H. Lacy at Cortland Academy, HomerNovember243 11
Scope and Contents
"Seward runs behind while Bouck runs far ahead of his ticket"; other political comments. Also, references to college life, studies, professors.From B. C. Whiting, Binghamton, to SRMarch 15, 1841243 11From O. S. Wood, Dryden, to SR at UnionMarch 25, 1841243 11Printed report form from Union; addressed to George RobertsonApril [6] 1841 (postmark)243 11
Scope and Contents
Includes tuition bill and marks for term January 8-April 1, 1841; also general information on courses, admission requirements, etc.From Ruel Smith, RochesterMay 21, 1841243 11
Scope and Contents
Who is studying medicine in the office of a professor of surgery at Geneva Medical College; mentions practices of medical students who procured corpses already buried for their dissections. To SR at Union.Term report from Union as in April aboveAugust 11, 1841243 11
Scope and Contents
For period April 30-July 28, 1841.From Cousin Molly, Albany, to SR at UnionDecember 9, 1841243 11January- October 1842 (11 items)From SR at Union College to William H. Lacy, CortlandvilleJanuary 8243 12
Scope and Contents
Remarks about newly elected state legislators; plans for present term at College and future entering of Law School.To SR at Union from N. B. Smith, IthacaFebruary 21, 1842243 12Term report from Union for period January 7-April 1, 1842April 9 (postmark)243 12From William Smith at Yale to SR at UnionMay 12 or 13 (sic)1842243 12From Howard Townsend, Albany, to SR at UnionMay 29, 1842243 12
Scope and Contents
Tells of H's journey to Washington, D.C.; his meeting John Quincy Adams, Daniel Webster, hearing Henry Clay speak, dining at the White House with President Tyler, and seeing the sights of the Capital.From James Wood, Jr., Union College, to SR in DryclenJuly 8, 1842243 12
Scope and Contents
About an overpayment of a debt; SR now in a lawyer's office.From Isaac Mott to SR at UnionJuly 12, 1842(postmark)243 12To SR from a cousin. Tells of the illness of his Aunt SallyJuly 25, 1842243 12Term report from Union for period April 29th-July 27, 1842August 10, 1842 (postmark)243 12From Cyrus Robertson in Albion, Michigan, to his brother, SRSeptember 5, 1842243 12
Scope and Contents
Concern about worth of Michigan land SR wishers to sellFrom Walter Mott, cousin of SROctober 12, 1842243 12
Scope and Contents
Invitation to SR to visit Mott in Albany at; the Medical College and perhaps see an operation performed.January 29 - October 7, 1843 (9 items)From Howard Townsend at Union to SR in EtnaJanuary 29, 1843243 13From Mitchell (fraternity brother of SR?), "Ike" and others to SRFebruary 1843243 13From N. R. Biddlecomb, and others to SRMarch 31, 1843243 13
Scope and Contents
Urging his return to Union College and reporting events since his departure.From State of New York, Secretary's Office, Department of Common Schools Albany to SR- -Superintendent of Common Schools, Tompkins Co., IthacaApril 10, 1843243 13
Scope and Contents
Regarding a law of 1843 stating that all appeals presented to the State Secretary's Office must have first been submitted to and had a decision rendered by the superintendent of schools in the particular county.From M. Scofield, Whitesboro, to SRApril 16, 1843243 13From SR at Union College to parentsMay 1, 1843243 13
Scope and Contents
Regarding his return to CollegeFrom D. R. Houts, Ithaca, to SR at Union CollegeMay 23, 1843243 13From Hiram D. Robertson at Albion, Michigan, to Parents (George Robertson) about trip to DetroitMay 23, 1843243 13From Asa Barton, Union College to SR at Varna, Tompkins CoOctober 7, 1843243 13
Scope and Contents
About news of SR's fraternity at Union, Sigma Phi.January 9 - April 16, 1844 (11 items)From W. Smith at Yale University to SR, Varna, Tompkins Co., N.YJanuary 9, 1844243 14
Scope and Contents
Some facts about life at Yale and news of friendsFrom S. Town of Aurora to SRJanuary 18, 1844243 14
Scope and Contents
About Town help including Robertson with a "pioneer enterprise thro' which the cause of primary education will receive so much aid."From H. Gillam of Aurora to SR about the possible visit of a Mr. TownFebruary 15, 1844243 14From S. Town of Aurora to SRFebruary 27, 1844243 14
Scope and Contents
Says that a visit to Tompkins Co. would be impossible. Town speaks of a project undertaken by Tompkins Co. teachers that has spread to other parts of the State; he commends the venture and wishes them success in the future.From A. Potter, Union College, to SR, IthacaMarch 6, 1844243 14
Scope and Contents
Potter isn't sure whether or not he will be able to attend an Institute.From S. Young, Secretary's Office, Department of Common Schools, Albany to SR, Superintendent of Tompkins Co. Common SchoolsMarch 11, 1844243 14
Scope and Contents
Regarding teacher certification for a Mr. Curtis and commending SR on the reputation of the Teachers' InstituteFrom D. A. Morton, town clerk, Groton, to SR, Superintendent Common Schools of Tompkins CountyMarch 11, 1844243 14
Scope and Contents
Listing names of district clerks for the Groton Common Schools.From Eleaser Carter of Caroline, to SRMarch 20, 1844243 14
Scope and Contents
Informing SR that the trustees have refused his (SR's) request for a meeting. Carter says that he will arrange for SR to review the clerk's books and will give SR all the help that he can.From Cyrus Clark and John S. Holden to SR as Superintendent of Tompkins Co. Common SchoolsMarch 21, 1844243 14
Scope and Contents
A notice of an appeal being sent to the N.Y.S. Superintendent of Schools concerning the Board of Trustees' refusal to comply with an existing statute. SR. had ruled in favor of the Trustees.George B. Glendening, Cayuga Academy, Aurora to SRMarch 27, 1844243 14
Scope and Contents
Acceptance of invitation to visit SR.From S. Young, State of New York Secretary's Office, Dept of Common. Schools, to SRMarch 30, 1844243 14
Scope and Contents
State Superintendent upholds decision of SR in the case of Holden and Clark vs. the Trustees of the town, of Lansing District #7, saying that trustees have right to levy tax.From Douglass Boardman, Trumansburg, to SRApril 3, 1844243 14
Scope and Contents
Letter tells of the electioneering done by Boardman's opponents in an election in the attempts to get another man elected.From S. Town, Angelica, to SRApril 16, 1844243 14
Scope and Contents
Reports on the Teacher's Institute in Angelica.May 27 - December 31, 1844 (14 items)To SR from. S.D. Carr of GrotonMay 27, 1844243 15
Scope and Contents
A man in the school district refuses to pay taxes on land which is not in said district.From Douglass Boardman, Trumansburg, to SRJune 4, 1844243 15
Scope and Contents
Requesting that SR send the dates he will be able to visit Trumansburg.Printed invitation from Alpha of the Sigma Phi with note from How(ard?) PorterJune 29, 1844243 15To SR from S. D. CarrJuly 29, 1844243 15
Scope and Contents
Asking that SR visit the Groton district schools and commenting on the next school term.From Lyman Cobb, N.Y.C. to SRAugust 12, 1844243 15
Scope and Contents
About text books being considered by the Books Committee of the County School Association.From S. Young, Secretary's Office, Dept. of Common Schools, Albany, to SRAugust 13, 1844243 15
Scope and Contents
Granting the request for an extension in handing in the district's annual report and commending the evaluation SR is doing of the Common Schools in Tompkins Co.From Douglass Boardman, Trumansburg, to SRAugust 13, 1844243 15
Scope and Contents
The State Superintendent has asked that each district send in the number and description of the books in the school libraries. Boardman also reports that a district meeting will soon take place to discuss new school sites and taxes.From S. Young, Secretary's Office, Department of Common Schools, Albany to SRSeptember 4, 1844243 15
Scope and Contents
The state regulation calling for a catalogue of books held by each district must be followed.To SR from D. W. Carley, NewfieldSeptember 21, 1844243 15
Scope and Contents
Carley tells of his plans for the ending of the school term and the examination of his pupils.From S. Town to SR. Town "will come to Ithaca."October 3, 1844243 15From E. Powell, Owego, to SROctober 17, 1844243 15
Scope and Contents
Letter sends news of Teacher Institute.From S. D. Carr, Groton, to SROctober 21, 1844243 15
Scope and Contents
Letter about the possible hiring of a Mr. Everson in SR's district.From Alex Holland, Union Coll., to SRDecember 3, 1844243 15
Scope and Contents
Acknowledgment payment of money to Union College.From Horace Mann, Boston, to SRDecember 31, 1844243 15
Scope and Contents
Can not meet with SR until following April. Commends SR for his (SR's) new work in New York State common schools.January 10 - July 18, 1845 (15 items)From P. A. Ely, Hector, to SR, Superintendent Tompkins Co. Common SchoolsJanuary 10, 1845243 16
Scope and Contents
A request of SR can not be fulfilled and Ely does not, have a candidate for the Normal School.From Robert Alien, Joseph Ammack, Samuel Alien, Jonathan Owen, Woodard White, and John Fish, all of Enfield, to SR, Superintendent of Tompkins Co. Common SchoolsJanuary 15, 1845243 16
Scope and Contents
Letter is an appeal to SR in behalf of a poor family in the school district. The District Board of Trustees has demanded the payment of the rate bill by this man who does not have the money. The petitioners are asking SR to exempt this man from the rate bill.From S. Town of Aurora to SR, Supt. Tompkins Co. Common SchoolMarch 8, 1845243 16
Scope and Contents
S. Town can not accept invitation of SR.From E. G. Starke, Auburn, to SRMarch 10, 1845243 16
Scope and Contents
A letter which SR mailed to Starke had been sent to the wrong address and the event which Starke had been invited to, had already taken place. Starke is angered by a movement in Albany seeking to abolish County Superintendents. He attacks Silas Wright, U.S. Senator 1833; reelected 1837 and 1843; governor 1845-47, as being the American Cato and states that Wright has not the information needed to make the statements he has been making. Starke comes out against "bigotry and ignorance coupled with rank injustice in high stations."From Edwin Cooper, Waterloo Academy, to SRMarch 15, 1845243 16
Scope and Contents
Cooper can not "during his vacation" visit SR but will be in Ithaca for two days during the next institute. He praises SR's work and comments on the effect it has upon other counties.From S. Young, Ballston, to SR, IthacaMarch 25, 1845243 16
Scope and Contents
The letter is an answer to an invitation of SR to Young. Young talks about the prejudices of certain segments of the community against the school system. Young says that education is not for the rich alone, as the mass of people think, but for the poor as well. No one should be denied the opportunity of receiving an education. '"No philanthropist should remit his exertions until our Schools are raised to such a grade of utility as to embrace all the children of the State, the sons and daughters of the rich as well as of the poor. All should here mingle on one common level; all should be blended into one mass during the susceptible period of youth. This is the only means of promoting the harmony of society, of suppressing jealousies and perpetuating in letter and in spirit our republican institutions."From Edwin Cooper, Waterloo to SR, IthacaApril 7, 1845243 16
Scope and Contents
Cooper will not be able to attend the Teacher Institute in Ithaca because of the illness of his wife.From Cyrus Robertson, Albion, Michigan, to his brother Smith Robertson, IthacaMay 4, 1845243 16>Lovel (?) Preston, Caroline, to SRMay 30, 1845243 16
Scope and Contents
Letter is praising SR for his system of school instruction.Printed folder - written by Franklin KnightJuly 1, 1845243 16
Scope and Contents
The folder is a sort of advertisement for a book of facsimiles of letter from George Washington on agriculture Knight has published that will "have a most salutary influence in correcting, in the youth of our country, many false notions regarding the character of agricultural pursuits."Letter from the publisher, Alexander V. Blake, N.Y.C. to SRJuly 1, 1845243 16
Scope and Contents
Printed circular for Wickham's "Educational Incentives," particularly Teacher's Tokens with facsimile. Letter tells SR about the invention of a School Ledger which would enable a teacher to keep permanent records of students.From I (?) Cooper McLean, N.Y. , to SRJuly 2, 1845243 16
Scope and Contents
A new certificate is to be made out for Miss Samantha Burton enabling her to teach in any school district in Tompkins' County.R. Hyde Walworth, President, New York Juvenile Temperance Association to SR and all County and town superintendentsJuly 4, 1845243 16
Scope and Contents
Printed circular urging them to promote said association and its goals in their district or county.From Francis Dwight, Albany, to SRJuly 7, 1845243 16
Scope and Contents
A Mr. D. P. Page will be in the area in the middle of September and will be willing to address a Teacher Institute if there is one at that time.From Henry McGonegal of AlbanyJuly 18, 1845243 16
Scope and Contents
Asking SR for a recommendation. McGonegal is seeking a position in Syracuse as a teacher.August 11 - November 13, 1845 (10 items)From Albert D. Wright, Verona to SR, IthacaAugust 11, 1845243 17
Scope and Contents
Dwight had promised SR that he would speak at the Tompkins County Teachers' Institute. He is asking to be released from this engagement since he is being asked by his own district (county) to be in charge of an Institute there.From E. C. Seymour, Albany, to SR, IthacaAugust 11, 1845243 17
Scope and Contents
Letter informs that Seymour will be leaving the school at the end of the term. Professors Perkins and Page will not be able to attend the Tompkins County Institute. Seymour asks the help of SR in finding a job for the next term.Circular giving details of the coming Cayuga County Teachers' InstituteAugust 13, 1845243 17From Henry Barnard of Providence to SR, IthacaSeptember 14, 1845243 17
Scope and Contents
Barnard will be able to attend the Institute and will deliver an address on "What can be done to improve the schools this winter?"From Henry Barnard,Providence, to SR, IthacaOctober 5, 1845243 17
Scope and Contents
The letter confirms Barnard's appearance at the Teachers' Institute, October 14 and announces his plans for an earlier arrival.From Edwin Cooper, Syracuse, to SR, IthacaOctober 22, 1845243 17
Scope and Contents
Cooper will not be able to attend the Teachers' Institute. He also encourages SR to accept a copy of a "current volume of the Advocates'" and to encourage the teachers in his district to become subscribers.From O. O. Wickham, N.Y.C. to SROctober 27, 1845243 17
Scope and Contents
Wickham will not be able to attend the Institute and present his "Educational Incentives" to the teachers present. However, he is sending samples and asks that a wall chart called School Chart of Characteristics be placed on the wall of some room at the Institute.From Charles R. Coburn, Owego, to SROctober 30, 1845243 17
Scope and Contents
Inviting SR and Mr. Barnard to the Owego Institute and Esq. Gillet, if he can be spared. He invites SR to speak to them about education.From Daniel Jackson, Burdett, to SRNovember 8, 1845243 17
Scope and Contents
About a teacher, Mr. J. W. Carpenter, then teaching at his school, who had the previous year been in trouble over a young woman of the town whom he had since married. Jackson says that except for that one incident, Carpenter's reputation and behavior had been very fine.From O. O. Wickham, N.Y.C. to SRNovember 13, 1845243 17
Scope and Contents
Asking for the names of all of those present at the last Teachers' Institute so that he may advertise his Teacher's Book of Instrumentalities and School Requisites.January 27 - July 28, 1846 (12 items)From Thomas Slater, Albany, to SRJanuary 27, 1846243 18
Scope and Contents
Professor Perkins will be able to attend the Institute planned for the spring.From H. Camp, school trustee in Trumansburg, to SR, Supt. of Common Schools, Tompkins CountyFebruary 17, 1846243 18
Scope and Contents
In regard to a notice that there will be a public examination of students in the near future. Camp says, on behalf of the other trustees, that their school will not be taking part in the examination.From C. S. Ford, Geneva to SRMarch 19, 1846243 18
Scope and Contents
About Ford's appearance as a lecturer at a future Teachers' Institute.From S. Town, Aurora to SRMarch 26, 1846243 18
Scope and Contents
Town cannot, at the time, indicate whether or not he will be able to visit Ithaca.From G. H. Stevens, Danby, to SRApril 8, 1846243 18
Scope and Contents
Sherman Miller has been newly elected Town Superintendent of Schools. Canfield and Stevens are going to School [to keep school?]From Doug(lass) (Boardman) Trumansburg, to SR as Superintendent of Common Schools, Tompkins CoMay 20, 1846243 18
Scope and Contents
Giving exact time of his arrival in Ithaca.SR from T. Russell Jencks of BostonMay 26, 1846243 18From Palmer and Jencks, Boston, to SR, Supt. of Common SchoolsJune 15, 1846243 18
Scope and Contents
They have found the journals he enquired about and wish to know if he wants them to be sent to him.Printed circular from N. S. Benton, Supt., N.Y.S., Secretary's Office, Dept. of Common SchoolsJuly 1, 1846243 18
Scope and Contents
A previous circular should be disregarded if it does not apply to a particular Superintendent,.From J. S. (or I. S.) Mitchell, Canajoharie, to SRJuly 10, 1846243 18From Superintendent of Caroline to SRJuly 28, 1846243 18
Scope and Contents
Reporting on the salary of the County and town superintendent of schools note attached re: amount of compensation for Dr. Farlin [See Ace. #2241]. Town Superintendent.August 12 - October 26, 1846 (11 items)From Jacob Abbott of New York to SRAugust 12, 1846243 19
Scope and Contents
Abbott is not certain whether or not he will be able to attend an Institute scheduled for early October. He thanks SR for the positive information and says that he will send a definite answer when he is able to.From C. La. (?) Ford, Medina to SR of IthacaAugust 15, 1846243 19
Scope and Contents
Ford is not certain whether or not he will be able to speak on Anatomy and Physiology at an Institute in the early part of OctoberFrom A.J. Tompkins of Lansing inviting SR to their school celebrationSeptember 11, 1846243 19From Alonzo Wynkoop, Chemung, to SR of IthacaSeptember 21, 1846243 19
Scope and Contents
Wynkoop asks for SR's assistance in finding qualified teachers to lead an Institute.From Horace Mann, Boston to SRSeptember 26, 1846243 19
Scope and Contents
Mann sends his regrets and says he will not be able to attend their Institute. He commends SR for the work he is doing.Printed circular from Andrews and Boyle, Publishers, N.Y.C. to SRSeptember 26, 1846243 19
Scope and Contents
Requesting the names and mailing addresses of all the town superintendents in the county and all of the teachers in the county. Andrews and Boyle are publishing a weekly newspaper, the Anglo Saxon, and hope to interest the superintendents and teachers in becoming subscribers.From Jacob Abbott, N.Y., to SROctober 5, 1846243 19
Scope and Contents
Abbott will be able to visit the Institute.Printed circular from 0. C. Gardiner of N.Y.C. to SR, Supt. of Common Schools, Tompkins CountyOctober 8, 1846243 19
Scope and Contents
Circular is a testimonial for the magazine American Quarterly Journal of Agriculture and Science; hand written letter {pages 2-3) is sent to encourage SR to subscribe to magazine.From Oliver Dyer of New York to SROctober 8, 1846243 19
Scope and Contents
Mr. Augustus Boyle will not be able to address 'the Teachers' Institute on Phonographics and sends his regrets.From P. S. Ely, Hector, to SR of IthacaOctober 16, 1846243 19
Scope and Contents
Ely's visit to Ithaca has been postponed a week. The Burdett School is in need of a teacher and Ely suggests that SR send them one.Form letter from N. S. Benton, Supt. Common Schools, Secretary's Office, Dept. of Common Schools, Albany to County Superintendent SROctober 26, 1846243 19
Scope and Contents
Blank reports for district trustees are available.February 19 - October 14, 1847 (11 items)Form letter from Delphian Institute of Union College, Schenectady to SR as a former memberFebruary 19, 1847243 110
Scope and Contents
Asking for a donation of either money or books to keep, in the Institute's attempt to fit up a reading room.From Alonzo Wynkoop, County Supt. of Chemung, to SR, IthacaMarch 15, 1847243 110
Scope and Contents
Three counties, Tompkins, Steuben and Chemung are planning a union Teachers' Institute to which all the neighboring counties are being invited. The letter from Wynkoop is to inform SR of what has been planned so far; who the principal speakers will be, a sample of the notice to appear in the newspapers (handwritten).From Alonzo Wynkoop, Chemung, to SR, IthacaApril 10, 1847243 110
Scope and Contents
Letter gives SR more news about the union Institute informing him that notices have been sent to newspapers. Wynkoop has had no definite answer from those whom he has asked to address the Institute, If all else fails, he will call upon SR to deliver an address. (first mention is made of Robertson being married as Wynkoop says " . . .and shall hope to meet your bride,. . . .")From N. S. Benton, State of New York Secretary's Office, Dept. of Common Schools, Albany to SRMay 6, 1847243 110
Scope and Contents
Letter says that land is liable to assessment by a district if it lies partially within that district and if the owner/occupant resides on the land in that particular district.From B. F. Winegar, a member of SR's fraternity, Sigma Phi, at Union College, to SRJune 23, 1847243 110
Scope and Contents
The fraternity needs donations from members to build a membership hall. This letter contains second mention of SR having recently married.From Cyrus Robertson, Albion, Michigan, to his brother, SRJuly 24, 1847243 110Printed circular from N. S. Benton, Secretary's Office, Dept. of Common Schools, Albany, to County Supt. SRSeptember 1, 1847243 110
Scope and Contents
The circular contains information pertaining to the reduction of the amount of gratuity allowed to pupils.From Benj[amin] Cook, Penn Yan to SROctober 4, 1847243 110
Scope and Contents
Cook will not be able to attend the whole of an Institute to lecture and teach Phonography but will be present for the second week.From J. S. (?) Holmes, Deputy Supt. of Common Schools, State of New York, Secretary's Office, Dept. of Common Schools, to SROctober 7, 1847243 110
Scope and Contents
Letter concerns a discrepancy concerning sums of money received during the current year, which appear in SR's abstract of Town Superintendent Reports.From James Redfield of Lyons, County Supt. for Wayne County to SROctober 14, 1847243 110
Scope and Contents
Introducing Dr. Calvin Cutler, author of a book on physiology; on same sheet.From Calvin Cutler, Ithaca to SROctober 19, 1847243 110
Scope and Contents
Sending SR a copy of the above mentioned book.January 16, 1848 - June 30, 1852From Philip and Sarah Robertson, Conneaut, Ohio, to SRJanuary 16, 1848243 111From B. R. King & P. R. King, Middleport, N.Y. to their brother and sister, SR and ? (his wife?) sleighing; RR correspondenceMarch 26, 1848243 111From W. Van Wyck, Union College to SRMarch 27, 1848243 111
Scope and Contents
The letter asks for funds for the finishing of the Conclave hall for the Sigma Phi fraternity at Union College.From H. (?) McGonegal, Baldwinsville, to SRJuly 11, 1848243 111
Scope and Contents
Asking SR for a recommendation for a teaching position.From O. O. Wickham, Clinton Hall (pm Williamsburg) , N.Y. to SRNovember 16, 1849243 111
Scope and Contents
Wickham requests a list of the names and addresses of the teachers in the area so that he may send them ". . .some valuable information which will be to their advantage and advance the cause of education."Printed circular from Association of Former Students of Cortland Academy to SRJune 30, 1852243 111
Scope and Contents
Asks for donations for a fund to be given to Mr. Wollworth, by his former pupils upon his resignation.Undated (5 items)From Thomas (?) Dwight, Albany, to SRJuly 17243 112
Scope and Contents
Concerns an Institute not being held. There is also mention made of an attack upon the present school system being made at a teachers' convention.From Thomas (?) Dwight, Albany, to SRAugust 2243 112
Scope and Contents
Dwight is asking SR for information of the upcoming Tompkins County Institute.From Peter Selover, Homer, to SRNovember 4243 112
Scope and Contents
The author of the letter had been promised a letter he did not receive. He is now appealing to SR for a certificate and/or a letter of recommendation.Printed circular advertising an arithmetic seriesUndated243 112Printed circular advertising Mitchell's Map EstablishmentUndated243 112Miscellaneous Letters and Receipts (not involving Smith Robertson) 1821 - 1850 (20 items)1821 - 1850From Adjt. General W. L. Marcy, Troy, to General Samuel Hatheway, HomerOctober 29, 1821243 113
Scope and Contents
General Marcy has been asked to give an opinion about whether or not officers who did not parade their companies on a certain required date should be punished. If they did not know that they were required to perform a duty, then they can not be punished for failing to fulfill the obligation. Note: Xerox copy placed in Hatheway Family Papers, Ace. #2281.Receipt for pay as teacher in District #4, town and county unnamed; signed by Israel RobertsApril 28, 1828243 113From Ebby Hyde, Farmer [Interlaken], to Doctor Frederick Hyde, Cortland VillageMarch 4, 1836243 113
Scope and Contents
Augustus (?) has just left Ebby Hyde's after a visit. Ebby says that he regrets that Frederick could not have been there at the same time as Augustus but says it was not possible. Ebby talks of a trip he is soon to start upon and says he is looking forward to Frederick's visit.From G. Woodruff, Buffalo, to Dr. Frederick Hyde, CortlandvilleJuly 14, 1837243 113
Scope and Contents
Personal letter containing family news and other items of gossip. Woodruff teases Hyde about his (Hyde's) "unpatriotic bachelorhood."From C. F. Woodruff, Ithaca, to father (step-father?, father-in-law?) P. Pelton of Monticello, N.YJuly 20, 1842243 113
Scope and Contents
Regarding trip by stagecoach from Monticello to Ithaca.From J. W. French of Albion, New York, to Mr. E. B. Walsworth, of Walworth, Wayne CoDecember 18, 1843243 113
Scope and Contents
Concerning a report to be made out re the Walworth Academy of which Walsworth is principal.From James S. McRie, Union College, to E. B.. WalsworththJanuary 22, 1844243 113
Scope and Contents
Asking for a teaching position at the Walworth Academy where Walsworth is principal.From George Loomis of Genesee Wesleyan Seminary, Lima, Livingston Co. to E. B. WalsworthFebruary 21, 1844243 113
Scope and Contents
Saying that visit to "Lyceum" at Walworth, Wayne Co. at present would not be possible.From Joseph Rosenkrans of Fort Plain to Mr. Walsworth of Union CollegeMay 30, 1844243 113
Scope and Contents
Request that Walsworth deliver sermon in place of Rosenkrans while he (Rosenkrans) must be away.From Caleb Clark, Truxton, to Dr. Frederick Hyde, Cortland (ville)September 30, 1845243 113
Scope and Contents
Clark is ill and wishes Dr. Hyde to prescribe some medicine for him.From John Foster, Union College, to E. B. Walsworth at Auburn Theological SeminaryMay 26, 1846243 113
Scope and Contents
In doubt as to date of Auburn Commencement and "propriety" of his [Foster's] addressing the body.From Silas C. Brown, W. Bloomfield, to his nephew E. B. Walsworth, Auburn, N.Y. Family mattersJune 12, 1846243 113From Rev. E. G. Townsend, Sackets Harbor, to E. Walsworth, Auburn. Personal news and commentDecember 17, 1846243 113From H. W. Parker, Mexico, N.Y., to E. B. Walsworth, Auburn Theological SeminaryMarch 25, 1847243 113
Scope and Contents
(Rev.) Parker wishes someone to substitute for him for four Sabbaths and asks Walsworth's assistance in securing someone.From Albert West, Troy to Ira G. FellowsNovember 7, 1847243 113
Scope and Contents
Will be 21 11/8 (b. 1826). Visit to N.Y.C. - theatre-going, attending Trinity Church.From Albert West, Troy, to Ira G. Fellows, Pulaski. Mutual friends and daily activitiesMay 7, 1848243 113From Henry L. Young of Avon, to Rev. E. B. Walsworth, Union Theological Seminary, N.YMay 8, 1848243 113
Scope and Contents
Is invited to assume Presbyterian pastorate at Avon. "This is missionary ground. . . ."From Albert West, Troy, to Ira G. Fellows. Personal newsNovember 5, 1848243 113From Albert West, Troy, to Ira G. FellowsFebruary 4, 1850243 113
Scope and Contents
Social news -- sleigh rides.From Albert West, Troy, to Ira G. Fellows, PulaskiApril 12, 1850243 113
Scope and Contents
Maple sugar making news from California; "'tell the boys to let well enough alone and stay at home."Miscellaneous Madison and Chenango County School District Records (3 items)Warrant from Almon Baker, trustee of School District #8, Cazenovia, Madison County ordering him to receive school taxesJuly 13, 1884243 114
Scope and Contents
Includes a list of taxable inhabitants and amounts they owe. 1 item (2 pcs.)Order to Tax Collector to pay wages earned by Addie Warner as teacher in School District #9, Town of Lincklaen, Chenango CountyNovember 18, 1895243 114A handwritten catalogue of books (15 vols.) belonging to School District #2, Town of LincklaenUndated243 114 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
